H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (Pebble) shows strong efficacy for over-stimulation (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong). Distribution shows 66% positive responses with a concentration in mid-to-high scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable improvement, settling within 5-10 minutes, and strong positive music feedback. YouTube views are moderate (30) but lack retention data. AutoGluon predicts a similar overstimulation zone score of 6.859, aligning with observed mean. The panel agrees on the strong signal with no substantial disagreements.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: When feeling overwhelmed or overstimulated, listeners commonly report feeling calmer and less anxious within 5-10 minutes, with strong positive feedback on the music.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (Rainfall) shows good efficacy for over-stimulation (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong). Distribution shows 65% positive responses with a spread across scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable improvement, settling around 10 minutes or immediately, and mixed music opinions. YouTube views are very low (5), and no AutoGluon deviation was noted. The panel consensus indicates a reliable signal for over-stimulation management.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Users find this protocol helps them feel more grounded when overstimulated, with many reporting noticeable improvement and settling within 10 minutes or immediately.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier

H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners report this protocol soothes nervous system overload, often feeling better within 5-10 minutes and appreciating the calming rhythm and pleasant music.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (Meadow) shows strong efficacy for anxiety and stress reduction (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong). The distribution indicates 63% of responses are 7 or above, with concentration in higher scores. Qualitative data reveals mostly self-reported listeners noting moderate to noticeable improvement, settling around 10 minutes, and positive music and rhythm feedback. Despite high efficacy, YouTube views remain low (7), suggesting undiscovered value. AutoGluon predicts a higher anxiety zone score of 7.838 and recommends cross-bucket routing with mood/energy protocols for potential enhancement. The panel agrees on the strong signal, with no substantial disagreement noted.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners consistently report significant reductions in anxiety and stress with this protocol, often feeling calmer within 10 minutes and appreciating the soothing music and rhythm.

sphere_commentary.overview:
HEALING and THRIVING show higher average outcomes than TRANSFORMING, with HEALING demonstrating the greatest consistency and largest sample size.
• HEALING: n=574, mean=6.98, std=1.77 (most consistent, largest sample)
• THRIVING: n=245, mean=6.96, std=1.72 (highest mean per some analyses)
• TRANSFORMING: n=181, mean=6.59, std=1.87 (greatest spread, expected from ascending-arc design)
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
HEALING protocols show the most consistent responses with low variance, THRIVING achieves the highest mean outcomes particularly in Focus & Clarity, and TRANSFORMING exhibits the greatest variability as expected from its ascending-arc design.
• HEALING: Lowest standard deviation (1.77) and highest consistency across subtypes.
• THRIVING: Highest mean scores (6.96) driven by Focus & Clarity protocols (e.g., T-FC-L3 at 7.63).
• TRANSFORMING: Highest standard deviation (1.87) with ASCENT protocols (e.g., X-MD-L3 at 7.15) outperforming DESCENT (e.g., X-MC-L1 at 6.75).
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols show anomalies requiring investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45): Consistent low efficacy and high variability.
• T-MB-L3 (n=14, mean=6.93): Analytically isolated and underperforming relative to maintenance protocols.
• High std in small-n protocols (X-MD-L3 std=2.38, T-MB-L2 std=2.03): Unstable estimates due to limited data.
• Cross-run drift in T-MB-L1, X-MC-L1, T-FC-L2: Suggests evolving response patterns.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Development signals suggest targeted improvements for several protocols.
• H-AX-L1: Increase outreach via adviser routing and YouTube promotion to improve visibility and settling times.
• X-MD-L3: Create an L2 variant with a revised ascent curve to reduce variability and stabilize outcomes.
• T-MB-L3: Investigate population mismatch and increase adviser-driven routing for clinical populations.
• X-MC-L3: Investigate redesign to address bimodal distribution and low mean via Hz progression or delta adjustment.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals evolving patterns and analytical isolation in specific protocols.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: Most protocols show similarity <0.85, indicating evolving interpretations or instability.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL CLUSTERS: Strong pairing of X-ME-L1 and X-ME-L3 (0.812); T-MB-L3 is analytically isolated (lowest mean similarity).
• Confidence: Embedding data sufficient for trend spotting but drift analysis benefits from longer-term validation.

Qualitative data indicates noticeable to significant improvement, settling within 5-10 minutes, and positive music feedback. YouTube views are low (7), and AutoGluon alignment is consistent. The panel notes the developing confidence due to sample size but agrees on the promising signal.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1: T-FC-L1 (Compass) shows moderate efficacy for focus and clarity (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful). Distribution shows 56% positive responses with a spread and some lower scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable and significant improvement, settling within 5 minutes or immediately, and mixed rhythm feedback (sometimes busy). YouTube views are very low (5), and AutoGluon predicts a higher focus zone score of 7.728. The panel notes the discrepancy but agrees on the moderate efficacy signal.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint) shows strong efficacy for focus and clarity (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful). Distribution shows 66% positive responses with a concentration in mid-to-high scores. Qualitative data indicates moderate to significant improvement, settling within 10 minutes, and mixed music opinions with some noting slightly busy rhythm. YouTube views are very low (4), and AutoGluon alignment is consistent with observed mean. The panel consensus indicates a reliable signal with no substantial disagreements.
by_protocol.T-FC-L3: T-FC-L3 (Summit) shows strong efficacy for focus and clarity (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful). Distribution shows 71% positive responses with a concentration in high scores. Qualitative data indicates high significant improvement, settling within 10 minutes, and strong music admiration. YouTube views are moderate (15), and AutoGluon predicts a similar focus zone score of 7.728. The panel agrees on the strong signal with no notable disagreements.
by_protocol.T-MB-L1: T-MB-L1 (Stone) shows good efficacy for maintenance and balance (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful). Distribution shows 64% positive responses with a concentration in mid-to-high scores. Qualitative data indicates moderate to noticeable improvement, settling around 20 minutes, and neutral-to-positive music feedback. YouTube views are very low (3), and AutoGluon predicts a similar maintenance zone score of 7.693. The panel notes cross-run drift in embedding data but agrees on the reliable efficacy signal.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: T-MB-L2 (Axis) shows moderate efficacy for maintenance and balance (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful). Distribution shows 59% positive responses with a spread and some lower scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable improvement, settling within 5-10 minutes, and neutral music feedback. YouTube views are very low (4), and no AutoGluon deviation was noted. The panel notes the high standard deviation for the sample size but agrees on the moderate efficacy signal.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: T-MB-L3 (Orbit) shows developing efficacy for maintenance and balance (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing). Distribution shows 71% positive responses with a concentration in high scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable and significant improvement, settling within 5 minutes, and mixed music feedback. YouTube views are very low (3), and AutoGluon predicts a similar maintenance zone score of 7.693. The panel notes analytical isolation in embedding data and moderate confidence due to sample size, but agrees on the promising signal.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: X-MC-L1 (Cavern) shows moderate efficacy for mind and clarity (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful). Distribution shows 54% positive responses with a concentration in mid-to-high scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable improvement, settling around 10 minutes, and strong music approval. YouTube views are low (8), and AutoGluon cross-bucket routing suggests relevance to anxiety protocols. The panel notes cross-run drift in embedding data but agrees on the reliable signal with no substantial disagreements.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: X-MC-L2 (Starlight) shows developing efficacy for mind and clarity (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing). Distribution shows 56% positive responses with a concentration in mid-to-high scores. Qualitative data indicates significant improvement, settling around 10 minutes or immediately, and strong music approval. YouTube views are low (12), and no AutoGluon deviation was noted. The panel consensus indicates a promising signal with moderate confidence due to sample size.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos) shows concerning efficacy for mind and clarity (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing). Distribution shows 50% positive responses with bimodal concentration at low and mid scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable improvement but also reports of worsened effects, settling around 5-10 minutes, and mixed music feedback. YouTube views are low (8), and AutoGluon cross-bucket routing suggests relevance to mood/energy protocols. The panel is divided on interpretation but agrees the signal warrants investigation into redesign or population mismatch.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: X-MD-L1 (Footpath) shows moderate efficacy for motivation and drive (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful). Distribution shows 45% positive responses with a spread across scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable improvement, settling around 5 minutes, and varied music opinions. YouTube views are very low (5), and AutoGluon predicts a higher motivation zone score of 7.876. The panel notes the discrepancy but agrees on the moderate efficacy signal with moderate confidence.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: X-MD-L2 (Trail) shows developing efficacy for motivation and drive (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing). Distribution shows 43% positive responses with a concentration in low-to-mid scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable improvement, settling around 20 minutes, and mixed music opinions with some noting slightly busy rhythm. YouTube views CC are very low (7), and no AutoGluon deviation was noted. The panel consensus indicates a developing signal requiring more data.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: X-MD-L3 (Switchback) shows promising but variable efficacy for motivation and drive (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing). Distribution shows 62% positive responses with a spread and some high scores. Qualitative data indicates moderate improvement, settling around 5 minutes, and mixed music opinions with some noting strong groove. YouTube views are very low (7), and AutoGluon predicts a higher motivation zone score of 7.876. The panel notes the high variability but agrees on the promising signal warranting an L2 variant for stabilization.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X-ME-L1 (Breeze) shows good efficacy for mood and energy (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful). Distribution shows 54% positive responses with a concentration in mid-to-high scores. Qualitative data indicates moderate to significant improvement, settling within 10-20 minutes, and strong music approval. YouTube views are low (8), and AutoGluon predicts a much higher low zone score of 8.230. The panel notes the discrepancy but agrees on the moderate efficacy signal with potential for enhancement via cross-bucket routing.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise) shows good efficacy for mood and energy (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful). Distribution shows 64% positive responses with a concentration in mid-to-high scores. Qualitative data indicates significant improvement, settling within 20 minutes or immediately, and strong music approval. YouTube views are low (8), and AutoGluon cross-bucket routing suggests relevance to low-energy contexts. The panel consensus indicates a reliable signal with no substantial disagreements.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 (Updraft) shows developing efficacy for mood and energy (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing). Distribution shows 56% positive responses with a spread and some lower scores. Qualitative data indicates noticeable to significant improvement, settling within 5-10 minutes, and mixed music opinions with some reporting no effect. YouTube views are very low (4), and AutoGluon alignment is consistent with observed mean. The panel consensus indicates a promising signal with moderate confidence due to sample size.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Listeners consistently report significant reductions in anxiety and stress with this protocol, often feeling calmer within 10 minutes and appreciating the soothing music and rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Users find this protocol helpful for reducing anxiety and stress, with many reporting noticeable improvement and settling quickly, though music opinions vary.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Listeners report this protocol helps with anxiety and stress, often feeling better within 10 minutes and appreciating the calming rhythm and pleasant music.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: During moments of acute distress, many listeners find this protocol provides significant relief within 5-10 minutes, with positive feedback on the music and rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: This protocol helps people coping with difficult emotions, with many reporting moderate to noticeable improvement and settling within 5-10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Listeners in crisis often report quick relief (sometimes within a minute) and mixed but generally positive experiences with the music and rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: When feeling overwhelmed or overstimulated, listeners commonly report feeling calmer and less anxious within 5-10 minutes, with strong positive feedback on the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Users find this protocol helps them feel more grounded when overstimulated, with many reporting noticeable improvement and settling within 10 minutes or immediately.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Listeners report this protocol soothes nervous system overload, often feeling better within 5-10 minutes and appreciating the calming rhythm and pleasant music.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Many listeners find this protocol helps them relax and prepare for sleep, often feeling ready for rest within 5 minutes and enjoying the music and rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: This protocol supports restful states, with many listeners reporting improved relaxation within 10-20 minutes, though some note no effect and neutral music feedback.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Listeners often use this protocol to unwind before sleep or during stress, frequently noticing improved calm within 5-10 minutes and positive music feedback.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: People use this protocol for creative work, often feeling settled and able to focus within a few minutes or up to 10 minutes, with positive music feedback.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Users report this protocol supports creative states, often feeling focused and balanced within 5-10 minutes, with strong positive reactions to the music.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Listeners find this protocol helps them enter calm, creative states quickly, often feeling focused within 5-10 minutes and appreciating the music.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Users often find this protocol helps with focus during work or study, with many reporting improved concentration within 5 minutes, though some note the rhythm can feel busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: This protocol is commonly used for focus during work or study, with many reporting improved concentration within 10 minutes and mixed opinions on the music and rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Listeners report this protocol significantly improves focus and mental clarity, often feeling better within 10 minutes and strongly appreciating the music.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: People use this protocol to support emotional balance during therapy or similar activities, often feeling settled within 20 minutes and finding the music pleasant or neutral.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Listeners report this protocol helps maintain emotional stability during daily activities, with many noticing effects within 5-10 minutes and neutral music feedback.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Users find this protocol helpful for emotional regulation, often reporting noticeable improvement within 5 minutes and mixed but generally positive feedback on the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Listeners often use this protocol to support clear thinking during focused tasks, with many reporting improved mental clarity within 10 minutes and strong approval of the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Users find this protocol helps with mental clarity when feeling stuck, often reporting improved focus within 10 minutes and strong positive reactions to the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Listeners' experiences with this protocol for mental clarity vary more than usual, with some reporting improvement and others noting no effect or worsening, suggesting it may work best for specific individuals.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: This protocol helps build motivation and energy, with many listeners reporting a gradual mood lift within 5-10 minutes and mixed reactions to the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Users find this protocol helpful for increasing energy levels, often feeling more motivated within 5-10 minutes or up to 20 minutes, with mixed music feedback.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Listeners report this protocol boosts motivation and energy, often feeling a noticeable increase within 5 minutes, though experiences vary more than usual.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Many listeners find this protocol improves mood and energy when feeling low, often reporting noticeable improvement within 10-20 minutes and strongly appreciating the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Users report this protocol provides a gentle mood lift, often feeling more positive within 5-20 minutes and strongly enjoying the music.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Listeners find this protocol supports mood regulation, often reporting noticeable improvement within 5-10 minutes, though experiences vary and some note no effect.
